questions.question: Explain the Latin-square design. How is it constructed? Derive the ANOVA table for a p x p Latin-square design including degrees of freedom and F-tests.
keyPoints: Definition and purpose of Latin-square design
Construction rule: each treatment once per row and column
SS partitioning: SST = SSR + SSC + SSTr + SSE
Degrees of freedom: p-1 for rows, columns, treatments; (p-1)(p-2) for error
Mean squares and F-statistics
Complete ANOVA table
Assumptions and limitations

questions.question: In a 4 x 4 Latin-square design with treatments A, B, C, D, the following data was obtained: Row 1: A=12, B=15, C=20, D=18; Row 2: B=17, C=22, D=14, A=10; Row 3: C=24, D=16, A=8, B=19; Row 4: D=13, A=9, B=21, C=25. Perform the ANOVA and test for treatment effects at 5% significance.
keyPoints: Compute row, column, and treatment totals
Calculate CF and all sums of squares
Determine degrees of freedom
Construct complete ANOVA table
F-test for treatments and conclusion

questions.question: Compare and contrast Latin-square design with Randomized Block Design (RBD). When would you prefer one over the other?
keyPoints: RBD controls one blocking factor; LSD controls two
LSD requires p = number of rows = columns = treatments
LSD has fewer error df than RBD
LSD is more efficient when two blocking factors exist
RBD is simpler and more flexible in group sizes

questions.question: State the advantages and limitations of Latin-square design. Explain the assumption of no interaction and its implication.
keyPoints: Advantages: controls two nuisance factors, fewer observations needed
Limitations: p must be equal for all factors, no interaction testing
No-interaction assumption: row, column, treatment effects are additive
If interaction exists, error term is inflated
Small error df for small p reduces test power

questions.question: Construct a 3 x 3 Latin square and explain how the correction factor method is used to compute sums of squares in a Latin-square ANOVA.
keyPoints: Construction of a valid 3 x 3 Latin square
CF = G^2 / N where N = p^2
SST = Sum of x^2 - CF
SSR = Sum of (Row Total)^2 / p - CF
SSC = Sum of (Col Total)^2 / p - CF
SSTr = Sum of (Treatment Total)^2 / p - CF
SSE = SST - SSR - SSC - SSTr
